Home / SaaS Valuation Calculator

SaaS Valuation Calculator

Estimate your SaaS company valuation based on ARR, growth rate, and profit margin. See how different multiples and scenarios affect your exit value.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your annual recurring revenue (ARR).
2
Enter your year-over-year growth rate as a percentage.
3
Enter your profit margin as a percentage.
4
Review your valuation range: conservative, base case, and optimistic.
5
Check your estimated exit value based on your profit and growth.
6
Scroll down to see 5 valuation scenarios at different revenue multiples.

Frequently Asked Questions

How are SaaS companies valued?

SaaS companies are typically valued on a revenue multiple basis, usually 3-10x ARR. The exact multiple depends primarily on growth rate (the #1 driver), followed by profit margin, market size, churn rate, and competitive moat. Public SaaS companies trade at 5-15x revenue on average.

What is the Rule of 40 in SaaS valuation?

The Rule of 40 states that your growth rate + profit margin should equal 40% or more. If you are growing 60% and have a -20% margin, your score is 40 — you pass. If you are growing 15% with a 30% margin, your score is 45 — you also pass. Companies above 40 command premium multiples.

Does profitability matter for SaaS valuation?

Yes, increasingly so. From 2022 onward, the market shifted from "growth at all costs" to "efficient growth." Profitable SaaS companies now command 1-3x higher revenue multiples than unprofitable ones. Aim for at least 10-20% profit margins to maximize your multiple.

What is my SaaS actually worth to a buyer?

Valuation is theoretical — what matters is what someone will pay. For solopreneur SaaS businesses under $1M ARR, expect 2-4x SDE (Seller Discretionary Earnings), not ARR multiples. Marketplaces like Acquire.com and MicroAcquire show real transaction data. Most micro-SaaS sells for 2.5-3.5x annual profit.

How can I increase my SaaS valuation?

Focus on four levers: (1) Accelerate growth — even 10% improvement in growth rate can add 1-2x to your multiple. (2) Improve profit margins — cut costs, raise prices. (3) Reduce churn — lower churn means more predictable revenue. (4) Diversify revenue — multiple customer segments or products reduce buyer risk and increase multiples.

Results

Copy All
#1 💰 SaaS Valuation Estimate • Annual Revenue (ARR): $200,000 • YoY Growth Rate: 50.0% • Profit Margin: 25.0% • Annual Profit: $50,000 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 📊 Valuation Range (Revenue Multiple): • Conservative (6.0x): $1,200,000 • Base Case (8.0x): $1,600,000 • Optimistic (12.0x): $2,400,000 • Estimated Exit Value: $600,000 ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 🚀 You have high-growth SaaS metrics. Your multiple range reflects the premium that high-growth companies command. Focus on sustaining growth while improving margins. 💡 Tip: SaaS companies typically sell for 3-10x ARR. Growth rate is the #1 driver of multiples. A company growing 100% YoY can command 10-20x, while a flat company might sell for 2-3x. Profitability matters too — profitable SaaS companies get 1-3x premium.
#2 Distressed Sale (2x): $200,000 ARR × 2x = $400,000 valuation
#3 Flat Growth (4x): $200,000 ARR × 4x = $800,000 valuation
#4 Steady Growth (6x): $200,000 ARR × 6x = $1,200,000 valuation
#5 High Growth (10x): $200,000 ARR × 10x = $2,000,000 valuation
